editionChange.reason: Josh's explicit decision: the app's KJV column should read in familiar modern (1769 Blayney-standard) spelling, not 1611 original spelling. This reverses the prior convention documented in documentation/BIBLICAL_REMEDIATION_METHODOLOGY.md §1, which had been inferred from the app's pre-existing data rather than requested by Josh.
editionChange.scopeNote: This is a corpus-wide change, not book-specific. Genesis's KJV column was redone under this new source as the first application; every subsequent book (starting with Exodus) uses this same source going forward.

source.label: KJVA: King James Version (1769) with Strongs Numbers and Morphology and CatchWords, including Apocrypha (without glosses)
source.upstreamProvenance: Converted from the Sword Project's KJVA module (crosswire.org) via the repo's sword_to_json.py conversion script.
source.canonNote: 66 protocanonical books + 14 traditional KJV Apocrypha books (1&2 Esdras, Tobit, Judith, Additions to Esther, Wisdom, Sirach, Baruch, Prayer of Azariah, Susanna, Bel and the Dragon, Prayer of Manasses, 1&2 Maccabees). Does NOT cover 1 Enoch, 3/4 Maccabees, or Letter of Jeremiah as separate files -- those belong to other canon traditions and are sourced elsewhere in this project.

editionVerification.method: Landmark-verse check before trusting any diff, per standing project discipline.
editionVerification.check: Exodus 1:1 reads 'Now these are the names... every man... came with Jacob' -- modern spelling, not 1611 ('Nowe... euery man... Iacob'). Confirms this is the 1769 Blayney-standard text, not another orthography.

usagePattern: Full-column replacement per documentation/BIBLICAL_REMEDIATION_METHODOLOGY.md §4: replace the entire KJV column for a book from this source, matched by chapter:verse, rather than hand-patching. Re-verify at zero mismatches after writing.
